call_sequence: STEP 1 (recon): Recover selectors from bytecode and match against withdraw/claim/sweep/redeem/migrate patterns
STEP 2 (recon): Check proxy slots; if proxy, fetch implementation and repeat selector recovery
STEP 3 (recon): Replay recent transactions to identify which selectors are live and what arguments they accept
STEP 4 (hypothesis only): If a public value-moving selector with no caller check is found, call it with attacker-controlled recipient to drain token balance
attacker_path_hypothesis.expected_gain: Unknown until selectors are recovered. Upper bound is ~$174M in token value if a public unchecked sweep or withdraw selector exists.
preconditions: Contract holds ~$174M in token value (confirmed by tvl_usd tag evidence)
At least one public selector exists that moves tokens out of the contract
The selector either has no access control or uses a broken/missing check

live_context_required.name: source_code_and_verification_status
live_context_required.reason: verified_source is null — we do not know whether source is available on BSCScan. Source determines whether we can read access control, entitlement logic, or value-moving paths directly.

live_context_required.command_or_source: cast code 0xc0021e0849fadefb98761f40829009905dbd8ee8 --rpc-url $BSC_RPC | python3 -c 'import sys; b=bytes.fromhex(sys.stdin.read().strip()[2:]); [print(b[i+1:i+5].hex()) for i in range(len(b)-4) if b[i]==0x63]'
live_context_required.name: bytecode_and_selector_recovery
live_context_required.reason: No selectors are known. Bytecode must be fetched and PUSH4 patterns extracted to recover candidate function selectors, then matched against 4byte.directory to identify value-moving functions (withdraw, claim, redeem, sweep, migrate, etc.).

live_context_required.name: proxy_slot_check
live_context_required.reason: Unknown whether this is a proxy. If EIP-1967 implementation slot is populated, the real logic lives elsewhere and the proxy may expose a storage mismatch or newly reachable value-moving code.

live_context_required.name: deployer_address_and_creation_tx
live_context_required.reason: deployer_address is empty. Deployer identity is needed to check for closed-project links, funding cluster membership, and whether the same deployer controls other funded contracts.

live_context_required.name: recent_transaction_calldata_patterns
live_context_required.reason: volume24h_usd and liquidity_usd are both 0. Recent txlist calldata reveals which selectors are actually being called, who the callers are (EOA vs contract), and whether any privileged sweep or withdrawal activity has occurred.

live_context_required.name: token_balance_breakdown
live_context_required.reason: high_token_balance tag is set but the specific tokens, amounts, and depositor history are unknown. Token identity determines whether the balance is user-deposited principal, protocol-owned liquidity, or an unrelated subsidy.

live_context_required.name: owner_admin_slot_check
live_context_required.reason: No owner or admin is known. If owner is an EOA with no timelock, privileged functions may be callable by a single key. If owner slot is zero or unset, access control may be absent entirely.

expected_assertions: attacker token balance after > attacker token balance before
contract token balance after < contract token balance before
no revert on the call from an unprivileged EOA
setup: BSC mainnet fork at current block
Recovered ABI or raw selector list from bytecode analysis
Attacker EOA with no prior interaction with the contract
transaction_sequence: Identify target selector (withdraw/claim/sweep/redeem) from bytecode recovery
Call selector with attacker address as recipient or beneficiary argument
Assert token balance of attacker address increased and contract balance decreased

source_or_selector_basis.evidence: Tags: hidden_high_value_contract, audit_hidden_contract, unknown_verification_status, fresh_contract_large_token_balance. No source, no ABI, no calldata patterns available in the brief.
source_or_selector_basis.why_it_matters: Without selectors we cannot confirm or deny the existence of public value-moving paths. Selector recovery is the mandatory first step before any exploit hypothesis can be formed.

source_or_selector_basis.evidence: entity_type is unknown_protocol with unknown_verification_status. No proxy tags are present but absence of evidence is not evidence of absence for a hidden contract.
source_or_selector_basis.why_it_matters: If the contract is a proxy with a live implementation, the implementation may expose selectors not visible from the proxy bytecode alone, and a storage layout mismatch could expose live funds.

suspected_exploit_families: missing access control on value-moving functions
proxy, implementation, or storage mismatch that exposes live funds
unauthorized withdrawal, redeem, unstake, claim, liquidate, release, sweep, or migrate
why_this_target_matters: A fresh, hidden, unverified-status BSC contract holding ~$174M in token value with no public protocol identity, no deployer attribution, no known selectors, and zero trading volume. The combination of large token custody, hidden status, and complete absence of source or selector evidence makes this a high-priority recon target before any proof work. If the contract exposes public value-moving selectors without access control, the value at risk is among the largest in the current queue.
